title: NaN
subtitle: Not a Number
goal: Understand what NaN means and when it appears.
explanation: It appears when JavaScript cannot produce a valid number.
NaN has the Number type, even though it actually represents an invalid numeric result.
Use Number.isNaN() to check for NaN.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine giving a calculator the task of calculating the word “Pizza.” It does not know what to do, so it returns the special value NaN.

remember: NaN appears after a failed numeric conversion.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners may think NaN is a string or an error. It is actually a special numeric value.
